title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but improper use or reliance on it without validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives, potentially exposing systems to unaddressed risks.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to verify its accuracy and reliability before integrating it into production workflows.
Ensure that the tool is used in conjunction with other established vulnerability management processes to avoid over-reliance.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback to stay informed about potential issues or improvements.

title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud infrastructure, potentially leading to data encryption, operational disruptions, and financial losses. Compromised CI/CD pipelines could also result in the propagation of malicious code across multiple environments.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) and restricting access to critical systems.
Conduct a comprehensive audit of cloud infrastructure for unauthorized access or suspicious activity, and apply the principle of least privilege to all accounts.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline or in a secure, immutable environment to mitigate the impact of ransomware attacks.

It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: A vulnerability was detected in toeverything AFFiNE up to 0.26.3. This issue affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was detected in toeverything AFFiNE up to 0.26.3. This issue affects the function allowDocPreview of the file /workspace/:workspaceId/:docId of the component Public Markdown Preview Endpoint. The manipulation results in authorization bypass. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Public Markdown Preview Endpoint of toeverything AFFiNE up to version 0.26.3 allows for an authorization bypass, enabling unauthorized users to potentially access or manipulate sensitive documents. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of malicious use.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of toeverything AFFiNE if available, or apply mitigations such as disabling the Public Markdown Preview Endpoint.
Implement strict access control measures and monitor logs for unauthorized access attempts to the affected endpoint.
Notify users and stakeholders about the vulnerability and advise them to avoid sharing sensitive documents until the issue is resolved.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Dromara MaxKey up to 3.5.13. Affected by this issue is the...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Dromara MaxKey up to 3.5.13. Affected by this issue is the function StrUtils.checkSqlInjection of the file StrUtils.java. Performing a manipulation of the argument filtersfields results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute SQL injection attacks, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or manipulation of sensitive information stored in databases.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Dromara MaxKey to a version higher than 3.5.13 if available.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to mitigate SQL injection risks.
Monitor systems for unusual database activity and apply web application firewalls (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

description: A weakness has been identified in Wavlink WL-WN570HA1 R70HA1 V1410_221110. This issue affects the function set_sys_adm of the file /cgi-bin/adm.cgi. This manipulation of the argument Username causes command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Once again the vendors acted very professional and confirms, "that the WN570HA1 firmware version R70HA1 V1410_221110 has been removed from our website."

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7690.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in Wavlink WL-WN570HA1 R70HA1 V1410_221110. Impacted...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Wavlink WL-WN570HA1 R70HA1 V1410_221110. Impacted is the function set_sys_cmd of the file /cgi-bin/adm.cgi. Such manipulation of the argument command leads to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Once again the vendors acted very professional and confirms, "that the WN570HA1 firmware version R70HA1 V1410_221110 has been removed from our website." This vulnerability o

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Wavlink WL-WN570HA1 allows for remote command injection via the set_sys_cmd function in /cgi-bin/adm.cgi. If exploited, attackers could execute arbitrary commands on the device, potentially compromising the network it is connected to. However, the vendor has removed the affected firmware version from their website, reducing the likelihood of new deployments.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of affected devices to the latest secure version, if available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and block access to the /cgi-bin/adm.cgi endpoint if possible.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to 23.0.2. This vulnerability affects...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to 23.0.2. This vulnerability affects the function dol_verifyHash in the library htdocs/core/lib/security.lib.php of the component Online Signature Module. The manipulation results in improper verification of cryptographic signature. The attack may be performed from remote. Attacks of this nature are highly complex. It is stated that the exploitability is difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to version 23.0.2 allows improper verification of cryptographic signatures in the Online Signature Module. This could potentially lead to unauthorized actions or data manipulation if exploited. However, the exploitability is stated to be difficult and attacks are highly complex, reducing the immediate risk.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Dolibarr ERP CRM to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Disable the Online Signature Module temporarily if it is not critical to operations, until a fix is applied.
Monitor systems for any unusual activity and review logs for signs of exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-7024 - A flaw has been found in rawchen sims up to 004f783b1db5ecdfad81c8fdc3b34171211112de. Affected by th
description: A flaw has been found in rawchen sims up to 004f783b1db5ecdfad81c8fdc3b34171211112de. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file sims-master/src/web/servlet/file/DeleteFileServlet.java of the component deleteFileServlet Endpoint. Executing a manipulation of the argument filename can lead to path traversal. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. This product does not use versioning. This is why information about affected and unaffected releases are unavailable. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit a path traversal flaw in the DeleteFileServlet endpoint, potentially leading to unauthorized access, deletion, or manipulation of sensitive files on the server. This could result in data breaches, service disruptions, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Immediately implement input validation and sanitization on the 'filename' parameter to prevent path traversal attacks.
Restrict access to the DeleteFileServlet endpoint to authorized users only and monitor logs for suspicious activity.
Apply a virtual patch or deploy a web application firewall (WAF) with rules to block path traversal attempts until a permanent fix is available.

title: CVE-2026-7023 - A vulnerability was detected in ByteDance coze-studio up to 0.5.1. Affected by this vulnerability is
description: A vulnerability was detected in ByteDance coze-studio up to 0.5.1. Affected by this vulnerability is the function ExecuteSQL of the file backend/domain/memory/database/service/database_impl.go of the component databaseTool. Performing a manipulation results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the ExecuteSQL function of ByteDance coze-studio up to version 0.5.1 allows for SQL injection, which could enable attackers to manipulate or exfiltrate sensitive data from the database. Since the exploit is public and can be initiated remotely, the risk of exploitation is significantly elevated.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of ByteDance coze-studio if available, or apply any available mitigations to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Implement input validation and sanitization on all user inputs interacting with the ExecuteSQL function to prevent malicious payloads.
Monitor systems for unusual database queries or unauthorized access attempts and apply web application firewalls (WAFs) to block SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-7018 - A vulnerability was determined in Datavane Datavines up to 13607645e14a4982468cfdbcf75c85cde63bae71.
description: A vulnerability was determined in Datavane Datavines up to 13607645e14a4982468cfdbcf75c85cde63bae71. The affected element is an unknown function of the file datavines-core/src/main/java/io/datavines/core/utils/TokenManager.java of the component JWT Token Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument tokenSecret can lead to use of hard-coded cryptographic key . The attack can be executed remotely. The attack requires a high level of complexity. The exploitability is described as difficult. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. This product implements a rolling release for ongoing delivery, which means version information for affected or updated releases is unavailable. This patch is called e540d6dc04e2e6ad11907fb655f3728a13e7b939. It is advisable to implement a patch to correct this issue. The project was informed of the problem early through a pull request but has not reacted yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7018.

title: CVE-2026-7016 - A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the compone
description: A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the component ushki Plugin. Performing a manipulation of the argument f_ushka_new/f_ushk results in cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. Upgrading to version 109.4 is recommended to address this issue. The patch is named 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The vendor was informed early about this issue. They classify it as a "Self-XSS". They deployed a countermeasure: "Nevertheless, we consider this a violation of secure coding standards. The lack of filtering via `htmlspecialchars()` has already been fixed in the latest patch to prevent incorrect data display."

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7016.
